_meta.source: Arumugam M, et al. (2011). Enterotypes of the human gut microbiome. Nature 473:174-180. DOI: 10.1038/nature09944
_meta.note: Genus-level relative abundances representing each enterotype centroid. Values are approximate means from the original paper's supplementary data. We compute Euclidean distance in CLR-transformed space to assign samples.

ET1.name: Bacteroides-dominant
ET1.description: Common in Western diets. Strong protein and animal fat metabolisers. Typically associated with higher meat consumption.

ET2.name: Prevotella-dominant
ET2.description: Common in plant-based and agrarian populations. Strong plant carbohydrate fermenters. Associated with high-fibre diets.

ET3.name: Ruminococcus-dominant
ET3.description: Mixed profile with elevated Ruminococcus and Methanobrevibacter. Often seen in older adults and populations with high diversity. Associated with mucin degradation capability.
